questions: fuel cell ar battery er difference ki?
answers: A fuel cell converts chemical energy directly into electrical energy through an electrochemical reaction, typically combining hydrogen and oxygen to produce electricity, water, and heat.
Fuel cell holo emon device ja hydrogen ar oxygen er chemical reaction theke directly electricity produce kore. Battery er moto charge korte hoy na — fuel dilei cholbe.
Unlike batteries that store energy, fuel cells generate electricity continuously as long as fuel is supplied. They're used in vehicles, backup power systems, and even spacecraft.
Fuel cells are highly efficient and clean. Toyota Mirai and Hyundai Nexo are fuel cell cars. They're also used in buses, forklifts, and stationary power systems.

feelings.question: fuel cell car ki?
feelings.answer: A fuel cell car is a highly efficient and clean electric vehicle that converts chemical energy directly into electricity through an electrochemical reaction, typically combining hydrogen and oxygen to produce water and heat. It's also used in buses, forklifts, and stationary power systems.
